data.narrative:
Kreios Space is a seed-stage aerospace company that has raised €8M to date. The Space & Aerospace sector comprises 23 documented peers, including 5 public companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies spanning SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace. The breadth of the sector contrasts sharply with the scarcity of direct comparables at Kreios's stage and scale.

Two standard valuation methods prove inapplicable. Peer-median analysis fails due to insufficient narrow-filter matches: while 23 same-sector peers exist, only 1 company occupies an adjacent stage, yielding an n-count below the minimum threshold of 3. Relying on an all-sector median would conflate disparate business models and maturity levels. Public-comp anchoring using the sector's median P/S multiple of 64.2x across 6 public tickers cannot proceed without disclosed revenue; private companies at seed stage rarely publish this metric.

The analysis encounters a structural constraint common to frontier aerospace: concentration of capital in a handful of late-stage unicorns (SpaceX, Blue Origin) and a long tail of underfunded entrants. Without applicable triangulation methods, no quantitative valuation range can be defensibly derived from the data on file. The key uncertainty is Kreios's revenue trajectory and unit economics relative to public-market benchmarks—data unavailable in the seed-stage context. Further fundraising rounds or disclosed operational metrics (launch cadence, customer contracts, burn rate) would be required to anchor a rigorous valuation.
